content.full_text:
May 10, 1976


Assemblyman Bill McVittie
P.O. Box 160
Chino, California 91710

Dear Assemblyman McVittie:

Your letter of April 27, 1976 to Mr. Daniel Lowenstein
has been referred to me for reply.

The campaign disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act require persons to report certain information,
including the street address, of contributors of $50 or more.
Since the statute does not specify home address, filers may
report the street address of either the home or business of
such contributors.

Please contact me if you have further questions.

Sincerely,


Nan Hambleton
Special Compliance Representative

NH:bw

bcc: Jeanne

The image appears to be a mostly blank page with only a few small marks or artifacts visible at the top (some circular shapes and small dots in the upper right corner). The rest of the page is white/blank with no legible text content to transcribe.

Assembly
California Legislature

mailing address:

COMMITTEES
AGRICULTURE
JUDICIARY

P.O. BOX 160
CHINO CALIFORNIA 91710
(714) 627-3536

REVENUE AND TAXATION

BILL McVITTIE
ASSEMBLYMAN, SIXTY-FIFTH DISTRICT
(POMONA AND WESTERN SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY)

VICE CHAIRMAN
REVENUE AND TAXATION COMMITTEE

April 27, 1976

Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman, State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Contributor's residential address

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

Committee campaign statements apparently require the
reporting of the home address of contributors.

One contributor recently indicated that he was
extremely reluctant to disclose his home address because of
security reasons. May his office address, where he is readily
available, be listed so that the security of his residence is
maintained?

Sincerely,

Bill McVittie
Assemblyman, 65th District

sv

Not printed at Government expense

[illegible] UFRI24 [illegible]
